title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Kava's Vanuatu origin
description: Tanna Vanuatu — kava's documented centre of origin ~3,000 years ago. Piper methysticum was selected by Ni-Vanuatu cultivators over millennia.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Kava's Vanuatu origin
description: Efate Vanuatu — primary nakamal culture. 'Nakamal' is now the pan-Pacific word for the evening kava gathering.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Austronesian migration carries kava east + west
description: Vanuatu — starting point for one of the longest-range plant migrations in human history.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Austronesian migration carries kava east + west
description: Fiji — kava becomes 'yaqona' here; tanoa ceremonial bowl is a Fijian invention.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Austronesian migration carries kava east + west
description: Samoa — 'ava ceremony integrates with matai chieftaincy and the Fa'a Samoa cultural system.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Austronesian migration carries kava east + west
description: Tonga — Tu'i Tonga sacred king ritual. Tongan kava ceremony is one of the most formally codified.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Austronesian migration carries kava east + west
description: Tokelau + Cook Islands — kava reached the easternmost extent of its cultural range.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Austronesian migration carries kava east + west
description: Hawai'i — 'awa (kava) was cultivated widely by kānaka maoli until missionary suppression in the 19th century.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Austronesian migration carries kava east + west
description: Indonesia — kava reached as far west as Borneo on the Austronesian migration.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Missionary / colonial suppression
description: Vanuatu / New Hebrides — 1800s European missionaries declare kava 'heathen'. Presbyterian and Anglican missions ban it.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Missionary / colonial suppression
description: Samoa — missionaries restrict kava ceremony, but it survives because it's embedded in chiefly governance.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Missionary / colonial suppression
description: Hawai'i — aggressive missionary suppression nearly extinguishes 'awa culture; only recent reclamation has revived it.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern Pacific diaspora + global export
description: Vanuatu today — world's largest producer of noble (ceremonial-grade) kava.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern Pacific diaspora + global export
description: Fiji — major exporter. Fijian diaspora in Australia and NZ maintains ceremonial tradition.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern Pacific diaspora + global export
description: Auckland NZ — largest Pacific Islander urban population in the world. Kava bars serve diaspora communities.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern Pacific diaspora + global export
description: San Francisco Bay — US Pacific Islander diaspora + growing kava bar scene.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern Pacific diaspora + global export
description: Florida — US kava bar heartland; Jacksonville has the highest density of kava bars in the world outside the Pacific.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: regional names
description: Tongan — 'kava'. This is the English borrowing-language.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: regional names
description: Fijian — 'yaqona' (pronounced 'yang-goh-nah'). Entirely distinct from the Tongan word.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: regional names
description: Samoan — ''ava'. Same root, different phonology.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: regional names
description: Hawaiian — ''awa'. Same root as Samoan 'ava; 'k'→'glottal stop' shift.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Port Vila Vanuatu — largest noble kava exporter.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Suva Fiji — second-largest exporter.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Sydney Australia — Pacific Islander diaspora import hub.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Jacksonville FL — global centre of non-diaspora kava bar culture.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Amsterdam — 2002 EU ban on kava (later partially lifted) collapsed the European market for a decade. Farmers hit hardest in Vanuatu (Lebot et al. 2015).
